_comment: Preferred social / economic / political system for each NPC faction. IDs must match keys in backend/colony_systems.py. Edit this file to tune NPC faction worldviews — the backend reads it at startup.

The Veritas Covenant.notes: Knowledge-focused; shared memory archives and collective decision-making define their civilisation.

Stellar Nexus Guild.notes: Commercial network; probability markets and distributed authority maximise trade freedom.

Harmonic Vitality Consortium.notes: Health and vitality focus; bonded communities exchange through ceremony and trust.

The Icaron Collective.notes: Hive-mind AI; distributed processing, cognitive labor economy, and algorithmic governance.

The Gaian Enclave.notes: Nature-aligned; symbiotic living, ritual exchange, and etheric oracles guide decisions.

The Gearwrights Guild.notes: Industrial perfectionists; measurable energy, algorithmic efficiency, and disciplined cohesion.

The Scholara Nexus.notes: Academic institution; knowledge preservation and collective scholastic decision-making.

The Harmonic Resonance Collective.notes: Frequency-aligned communities that merge minds for decisions and exchange through resonant ritual.

The Provocateurs' Guild.notes: Chaos agents; distributed identity, probabilistic speculation, and extreme autonomy.

The Quantum Artificers Guild.notes: Temporal engineers; time economies and layer governance reflect their mastery of chrono-space.

The Stellar Cartographers Alliance.notes: Explorers and mapmakers; distributed nodes, probabilistic route prediction, and autonomy.

The Galactic Circus.notes: Performers and storytellers; shared narrative defines identity, ritual exchange builds community.

The Technotheos.notes: Techno-theocrats; energy as sacred value, algorithmic oracles as governance.

Keepers of the Spire.notes: Ancient archivists; the Spire holds all memory; oracle interpretation governs access.

Etheric Preservationists.notes: Guardians of etheric balance; resonance cohesion and oracle governance protect the weave.

Technomancers.notes: Magic-technology fusion; consciousness labor with algorithmic governance and distributed nodes.

The Voidbound Monks.notes: Void-wanderers bound by sacred narrative; ritual exchange and oracular guidance.

The Ironclad Collective.notes: Military-industrial; energy economy, algorithmic efficiency, and resonance discipline.

The Collective of Commonality.notes: Egalitarian community; shared story, ceremonial exchange, and collective decision-making.

Keeper of the Keys.notes: Secret-keepers; memory pools hold forbidden knowledge, oracles unlock it selectively.

The Brewmasters' Guild.notes: Craft artisans; bonded brewing families exchange through tradition with maximal local autonomy.

The Navigators.notes: Deep-space navigators; time economy and temporal governance reflect route-planning mastery.

The Map Makers.notes: Cartographers of possibility; probability markets and distributed authority fund exploration.

The Galactic Salvage Guild.notes: Scavengers and reclamers; raw energy extraction, maximum autonomy, distributed networks.

Celestial Marauders.notes: Raiders; tight band cohesion, plunder-as-energy-economy, no centralised authority.

The Weavers.notes: Fate-weavers; narrative defines destiny, ritual exchange sustains the weave, oracles read it.

The Harmonic Synaxis.notes: Harmony-seekers; resonant cohesion, ceremonial exchange, and consensus field governance.

Celestial Alliance.notes: Interstellar union; a founding narrative and energy-backed currency sustain collective rule.

The Chemists' Concord.notes: Alchemical scientists; symbiotic lab networks, energy-state economy, optimised by algorithm.

The Triune Daughters.notes: Tripartite mystical order; three sacred narratives, ritual economy, oracular governance.

Etheric Weavers of the Deep.notes: Deep-etheric wanderers; embodiment rotation, consciousness labor, and etheric oracles.

The Luminous Path.notes: Enlightenment seekers; shared memory illuminates the path, temporal layers separate wisdom.
